mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, aggressive monetary tightening restricts market liquidity and forces equity valuation compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS surpasses 5.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The concurrent payroll growth and unemployment decline fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising household wealth and construction sector activity that propagate through supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production above 102.0 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates restored manufacturing capacity and easing supply constraints, triggering positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production levels above 102.0 during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.
